[BREAKING]  Attackers probe Citrix NetScaler for CVE-2026-3055 memory overread flaw  watchTowr and Defused Cyber reported active reconnaissance on Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway for CVE-2026-3055 on March 28, 2026, with CVSS score 9.3. The flaw, an insufficient input validation issue leading to memory overread, affects only systems configured as SAML Identity Providers. Citrix issued security patches on March 23, 2026, but watchTowr Intel reported on March 29 that reconnaissance activity is already visible across honeypot networks.  Unauthenticated attackers can leak sensitive data from appliance memory. Organizations can identify vulnerable configurations by searching for the string "add authentication samlIdPProfile" in Citrix administrative settings. Citrix discovered the flaw internally; no public proof-of-concept code exists. CVE-2023-4966 (CitrixBleed), a similar memory-leak vulnerability, was widely exploited in 2023 after disclosure.  Citrix vulnerabilities have shown rapid progression from reconnaissance to exploitation. Organizations running affected NetScaler versions—14.1 before 14.1-66.59, 13.1 before 13.1-62.23, and 13.1-FIPS/13.1-NDcPP before 13.1-37.262—should apply patches immediately.  — THE FORGE'S WEIGHT — Active reconnaissance signals imminent exploitation risk for SAML IDP configurations on NetScaler. watchTowr Intel detects probes testing authentication flows; once exploitation begins, response windows shrink to hours. Many critical infrastructure teams lack visibility into their SAML IDP status.

India’s LPG Supply Stress — A Quiet but Real Risk  India’s LPG story has always had one weak link — heavy dependence on imports, largely from the Middle East. It works smoothly in stable times, but the moment geopolitics heats up, the vulnerability becomes visible.  That’s exactly what is playing out now. Rising tensions in West Asia have started impacting key shipping routes, especially around the Strait of Hormuz. Nothing has “broken” yet, but delays, higher freight risks, and tighter supply movement are clearly building under the surface.  On the ground, there’s no visible panic for households so far — cylinders are available and the system is holding. But behind the scenes, adjustments have already begun. Industrial and commercial users are facing tighter allocations, logistics is getting strained, and the cost pressure is slowly creeping in.  The government, to its credit, is moving early — diversifying sourcing, pushing domestic output, and quietly prioritizing household consumption over bulk users. There’s also a renewed push towards alternatives like PNG, which could reduce dependency over time.  So how serious is this?  Right now, it’s controlled. But it’s also a reminder. India isn’t in a crisis yet — it’s in a stress phase. And if geopolitical tensions stretch longer than expected, this could evolve from a supply issue into a broader economic concern.  Bottom line: This isn’t just about LPG. It’s about how exposed India still is to global energy disruptions — and how quickly that exposure can show up when the world gets unstable....

왜 금방 없어질 가능성이 큰가?  테슬라의 OTA 업데이트 방식: 테슬라는 FSD(자율주행) 관련 보안·소프트웨어 잠금을 매우 적극적으로 막습니다. 과거에도 CAN 버스 조작, 서드파티 장치, 지역 제한 우회 등을 OTA로 빠르게 차단한 전례가 많아요. 이번 해킹의 취약점: 장치가 CAN 버스 신호를 “스푸핑(spoofing)”하는 방식이라, 테슬라가 FSD 소프트웨어에 추가 검증 로직이나 CAN 프레임 체크를 넣는 업데이트만 하면 끝납니다. (이미 TACC 체크를 우회하는 코드를 개발자가 숨겼지만, 테슬라는 더 깊은 레벨에서 감지할 수 있어요.) 현재 상황 (2026년 3월 29일 기준): 아직 테슬라가 공식적으로 차단하지 않았지만, 이 해킹이 한국·유럽·일본 테슬라 커뮤니티에서 급속도로 퍼지고 있어요. 테슬라는 이런 “대규모 바이럴”을 감지하면 즉시 OTA를 밀어붙이는 패턴입니다. (보통 1~4주 안에 첫 패치가 나오는 경우가 많음)  다만 완전히 사라지진 않을 수도 있어요  개발자(Michał Gapiński, Tesla Android)는 새로운 버전을 빠르게 내놓을 가능성이 큽니다. (과거에도 테슬라 업데이트 후 1~2주 만에 대응한 적 있음) 장치가 “플러그 앤 플레이”라서, 업데이트 후에도 다시 연결하면 작동할 수 있지만, 테슬라가 차량을 “벽돌(brick)”하거나 보증을 무효화할 위험은 커집니다.  결론: 단기적으로(며칠~몇 주 내) 테슬라 OTA로 막힐 확률이 압도적으로 높아요. 이미 FSD v14.2를 비공식으로 돌리는 영상이 퍼지고 있지만, 테슬라는 이런 걸 그냥 둘 스타일이 절대 아니에요. 사용 중이시라면 지금 당장 안전을 위해 빼는 걸 추천합니다. (법적·안전·보증 문제도 크니까요.)

A drone sighting in restricted airspace near Air Force One triggered an immediate security response at Palm Beach International Airport, prompting a temporary ground stop and scrambling helicopters to investigate. The incident unfolded just as Air Force One was preparing for departure with President Donald Trump aboard, according to multiple reports.   🚨 What triggered the ground stop: - A drone was reportedly spotted near the airport’s restricted airspace, close to where Air Force One was staged.   - A Delta Air Lines pilot informed passengers that the drone intrusion caused an immediate halt to all taxiing and departures.  - Helicopters were scrambled to locate and assess the object, consistent with standard protective protocols when the president is on the airfield.   ✈️ Status of Air Force One at the time: - President Trump was scheduled to depart from PBI on Sunday.   - According to reporting cited in the search results, Air Force One had not yet taken off at the time of the drone scare.   🛑 Airport impact: - Palm Beach International Airport issued a temporary ground stop while the airspace was secured.  - FAA’s system later listed delays attributed to “volume,” though the agency did not formally link those delays to the drone incident.   🔍 What’s still unclear: - No formal statement has yet been released by the FAA or the airport regarding the exact nature of the object or whether it was definitively confirmed as a drone.   - No reports so far indicate any further escalation or threat to Air Force One.

Tata Motors Passenger Vehicle   1. Fundamental Analysis: The "Tale of Two Companies" The Q3 FY26 performance (ended Dec 31, 2025) presents a stark contrast between its domestic operations and its global luxury arm.  The JLR Drag:  The Cyber Shock: The consolidated results were severely impacted by a cyber incident at      JLR, which halted production for nearly a month. This led to a wholesale volume drop of 43.4% YoY to 59,100 units.  Financial Toll: JLR reported an EBIT margin of negative 6.8% and a massive negative free cash flow of £1.5 billion.  The China Problem: JLR is facing structural headwinds in China, its most profitable market. Premium segment demand is shrinking, and intense competition from local new energy vehicles is eroding sales. The Domestic Resilience (The Silver Lining): Market Share Gains: The Indian domestic PV business is thriving. TMPV achieved its  highest-ever quarterly wholesales of ~171,000 units, growing 22% YoY, and      secured the #2 rank with a 13.8% Vahan market share.  EV Dominance:  EV volumes surged 50% YoY, maintaining an exit market share of 46%. The      penetration of EV and CNG models combined stands at a robust 43.6%.  Margin Expansion:  Despite commodity pressures, the domestic business improved its EBITDA  margin to 7.0%, generating a positive Free Cash Flow of ~₹300 Cr.  2. Technical & Valuation Setup  The Valuation Disconnect: TMPV is currently trading at a P/E of 18.6x and   a Price-to-Book ratio of 1.01x. This is exceptionally cheap for a  company with a 20% ROCE and 28.1% ROE. The market is pricing the stock as      if the JLR cyber incident and the China slowdown are permanent, terminal events, rather than temporary or cyclical headwinds.  The Demerger Discount: The recent demerger often creates "orphan  stock" dynamics where institutional algorithms temporarily dump the   spun-off entity. This technical selling pressure has driven the stock down      ~4.6% recently.  3. The Growth Catalysts (FY27 & Beyond) The "Sierra" Effect: TMPV launched the highly anticipated Sierra SUV,   securing over 70,000 bookings on day one. Management confirmed they are      actively ramping up supply chain and in-house capacity at the Sanand 2   factory to meet this massive demand pipeline.  JLR Normalization:  Management confirmed that Q4 production at JLR plants (Solihull and Nitra)  has fully normalized, and the order bank remains strong, particularly for  the Defender model.  Product Pipeline: JLR is preparing for a massive launch cycle, including the Range Rover      Electric and a new production Jaguar, which will shift the narrative from      legacy wind-downs to next-gen electrification.  4. The Risks: JLR Debt:     The consolidated net debt stands at a heavy ~₹39,400 Cr, almost entirely  attributed to JLR. Prolonged weakness in China could strain their      deleveraging timeline.  EV Competition:   While TMPV dominates the Indian EV market, rising competition and changing  subsidy structures (PLI dependence) require constant monitoring  Technical Analysis : The stock has just suffered another violent red month, breaking below the critical 61.8% Fibonacci "Golden Zone" (₹337). The momentum is heavily bearish, meaning you must look lower for the true concrete floor.  Where is the "Generational Buy" Zone? (Institutional Demand)  The Ultimate Target: The ₹260 - ₹290 Zone. Here is the structural logic explaining why institutions will defend this exact box:  The 2023 Macro Order Block:   Look back at your historical data for Q1 2023. Between  January and April 2023, the stock flatlined in a tight, massive accumulation base between ₹262 and ₹289. In April 2023, "Smart  Money" triggered an explosive breakout from this exact box, launching  the stock to its ₹811 ATH. Price is currently returning to the exact "scene of the crime" to mitigate those original buy orders.  Educational Content / No Buy - Sell Recommendation

sample_tweets.text: It's funny stuff. When you reside in the United States (and presumably Europe) you always read about the Russian Federation or Chinese government performing cyber state-sponsored operations. It makes us look like a bitch who is getting bullied.  But then you cross the pond on the internet and take a look around and you're like, "hehe ya, we're doing it too actually, our cybersecurity companies just dont discuss it"

Okay, before I make a silly post have some context. Rostelecom is the largest telecommunication company in Russia. If you're in the United States, Rostelecom is basically like their AT&T or Verizon. Anyway, Rostelecom has a Cyber Threat Intelligence division called "Solar Group".  Solar Group releases papers frequently on threats (specifically in the malware domain) targeting the Russian Federation.  I enjoy reading it because, as a person residing in the United States, my Threat Feed is usually threats facing people in the United States (or allies of the United States). Reading threats facing the Russian Federation I'm like, "oh no shit? yall too? lmfao das crazyyyy".  My absolute favorite though is reading papers from Chinese or Russian cybersecurity companies where they accuse the United States government of state-sponsored malware campaigns and the United States government is like, "pfffft? Me? No way, dawg. I'm A CHRISTIAN. You ARE THE BAD GUYS. We go to Church EVERY SUNDAY".  Then both the Russian Federation and Chinese government go like: ">:( u bitch"  But then they do the same thing to us, so it's whatever I guess. We're all doing silly shenanigans on the internet.  An example of the silly shenanigans is a Threat Actor who has compromised various law enforcement agencies in the Russian Federation. This Threat Actor is named "Eagle Werewolf" (what country uses the Eagle?).  Eagle Werewolf has been compromising law enforcement agencies in the Russian Federation, specifically exfiltrating data related to internal case files, active investigations, operational plans from law enforcement agencies, and any information on who the Russian Federation is actively investigating. Eagle Werewolf also appears to be attempting to map internal infrastructure and organization hierarchy (who is who in law enforcement, supervisors, general employees, etc).  That's weird. Why would this mysterious "Eagle Werewolf" want this information? Hmmmmm?
Also, I'm not a European, so I don't understand the humor, but I see a bunch of people blaming Romanians. As an American I do not understand it, but apparently this is big humor for Europeans
March 20th: 15 TONS of gummy candy stolen from semi-trailer in Germany  March 29th: 12 TONS of KitKat bars stolen from Truck leaving Italy en-route to Poland  Who are you people?
I'm not really a humorless piece of poop. I just wanted to do the math on the quantity of KitKats STOLEN.

"I'd eat them".  False. That is impossible.  1 KitKat weights approx. 45g.  1lbs is approx. 450g.  Approx. 10 KitKats in a lbs.  12 tons is 24,000lbs.  That is roughly 240,000 KitKats  1 KitKat is approx. 230 calories.  That is approx. 55,200,000 calories.  The daily recommended calorie intake for an adult is 2,000 calories.  55,200,000 calories is 27,600 days of ideal calorie intake, or roughly 75 years.
What the fuck is going on in Europe?  Who steals 12 TONS of KitKats? What do you even do with that many KitKats?

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 위협 환경은 AI를 활용한 공격 코드 자동 생성 및 취약점 탐색이 운영 단계(Operational Maturity)에 진입하며 공격 속도가 비약적으로 빨라졌습니다. 특히 Citrix NetScaler, F5 BIG-IP, Fortinet FortiClient EMS와 같은 네트워크 인프라 장비의 Critical 취약점을 타겟으로 한 Active Exploitation이 급증하고 있습니다. 또한, ClickFix 캠페인과 같은 사회 공학적 기법이 macOS와 Windows를 가리지 않고 정교하게 진화하고 있으며, 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)이 지속적인 위협으로 자리 잡고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Handala Hack Team (Iran-linked):** FBI 국장 개인 이메일 해킹 및 데이터 유출, Stryker 대상 Wiper 공격 수행.
*   **Red Menshen (China-linked):** BPFdoor 악성코드를 활용한 글로벌 통신사 대상 스파이 활동.
*   **Silver Fox:** 일본 기업 대상 세무/인사 관련 피싱 이메일 유포.
*   **ShinyHunters:** European Commission(Europa.eu) 대상 데이터 유출 공격.
*   **Larva-26002:** MS-SQL 서버 대상 ICE Cloud 스캐너 설치 및 랜섬웨어 유포.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **INC Ransom:** 대한민국 철강 제조 기업 대상 랜섬웨어 공격 확인 (AhnLab ASEC).
*   **피싱 캠페인:** 국세청 세무조사를 사칭한 피싱 메일을 통해 백도어 악성코드 유포 (이스트시큐리티).
*   **기타:** 국내 타겟 APT 공격 모니터링 결과, 2026년 2월 기준 다양한 유형의 공격 지속 확인.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-3055:** Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway, CVSS 9.3, Active Reconnaissance 진행 중.
*   **CVE-2026-4681:** PTC Windchill Product Lifecycle Management, RCE 가능, 주의 필요.
*   **CVE-2025-53521:** F5 BIG-IP APM, RCE로 격상, CISA KEV 등록 및 실제 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2023-32434 / CVE-2023-38606:** iOS Kernel Exploit (Coruna framework), Operation Triangulation에서 사용.

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **인프라 패치:** F5 BIG-IP 및 Citrix NetScaler 장비의 최신 보안 패치 적용 여부를 즉시 확인하고, 노출된 관리 인터페이스에 대한 접근 제어 강화.
*   **피싱 대응:** 국세청 및 세무조사 관련 이메일 수신 시 첨부파일 실행을 엄격히 금지하고, EDR 솔루션을 통해 백도어 프로세스 탐지 정책 활성화.
*   **공급망 보안:** FortiClient EMS 등 보안 솔루션의 취약점 악용 사례가 보고되고 있으므로, 관련 소프트웨어의 벤더 권고 사항을 확인하고 업데이트 수행.
